raw_response:
```python
# Check that all 8 possible 3-qubit outcomes are present
expected_outcomes = {'000', '001', '010', '011', '100', '101', '110', '111'}
assert set(counts.keys()) == expected_outcomes, f"Missing outcomes: {expected_outcomes - set(counts.keys())}"

# Check that probabilities are approximately uniform (each should be ~1/8 = 0.125)
expected_prob = 1.0 / 8
tolerance = 0.1  # Allow 10% deviation from uniform distribution

for outcome in expected_outcomes:
    prob = counts[outcome]
    assert abs(prob - expected_prob) < tolerance, f"Outcome {outcome} probability {prob} deviates too much from expected {expected_prob}"
```

```python
# Verify all 8 three-qubit computational basis states appear
expected_outcomes = {'000', '001', '010', '011', '100', '101', '110', '111'}
assert set(counts.keys()) == expected_outcomes, f"Missing outcomes: {expected_outcomes - set(counts.keys())}"

# Verify approximately uniform distribution (each outcome should be ~1/8 = 12.5%)
expected_prob = 1.0 / 8
tolerance = 0.1  # 10% tolerance for statistical variation
for outcome in expected_outcomes:
    actual_prob = counts[outcome] / shots
    assert abs(actual_prob - expected_prob) < tolerance, f"Outcome {outcome}: {actual_prob:.3f} vs expected {expected_prob:.3f}"
```

```python
# Verify all 8 three-qubit outcomes appear with approximately uniform probabilities
expected_outcomes = {'000', '001', '010', '011', '100', '101', '110', '111'}
assert set(counts.keys()) == expected_outcomes, f"Missing outcomes: {expected_outcomes - set(counts.keys())}"

# Each outcome should appear with probability close to 1/8 = 0.125
expected_prob = 1.0 / 8
tolerance = 0.05  # Allow 5% deviation from uniform distribution

for outcome in expected_outcomes:
    prob = counts[outcome] / shots
    assert abs(prob - expected_prob) < tolerance, f"Outcome {outcome} has probability {prob:.3f}, expected ~{expected_prob:.3f}"

# Verify total counts sum to shots
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
assert total_counts == shots, f"Total counts {total_counts} != shots {shots}"
```

```python
# Check that all 8 possible 3-qubit outcomes are present
expected_outcomes = {'000', '001', '010', '011', '100', '101', '110', '111'}
assert set(counts.keys()) == expected_outcomes, f"Missing outcomes: {expected_outcomes - set(counts.keys())}"

# Check that probabilities are approximately uniform (each should be around 1/8 = 0.125)
expected_prob = 1.0 / 8
tolerance = 0.1  # Allow 10% deviation from uniform
for outcome, count in counts.items():
    prob = count / shots
    assert abs(prob - expected_prob) < tolerance, f"Outcome {outcome} has probability {prob:.3f}, expected ~{expected_prob:.3f}"
```

```python
# Verify all 8 possible 3-qubit outcomes appear with approximately uniform probabilities
expected_outcomes = {'000', '001', '010', '011', '100', '101', '110', '111'}
expected_prob = 1.0 / 8

# Check all outcomes are present
assert set(counts.keys()) == expected_outcomes, f"Missing outcomes: {expected_outcomes - set(counts.keys())}"

# Check probabilities are approximately uniform (within 20% tolerance)
tolerance = 0.2 * expected_prob
for outcome in expected_outcomes:
    prob = counts[outcome]
    assert abs(prob - expected_prob) <= tolerance, f"Outcome {outcome} has probability {prob}, expected ~{expected_prob}"
```

```python
# Verify all 8 three-qubit computational basis states appear
expected_states = {'000', '001', '010', '011', '100', '101', '110', '111'}
observed_states = set(counts.keys())
assert observed_states == expected_states, f"Missing states: {expected_states - observed_states}"

# Check approximately uniform distribution (each state should get ~1/8 of shots)
expected_prob = 1.0 / 8
tolerance = 0.15  # Allow 15% deviation from uniform
for state in expected_states:
    observed_prob = counts[state] / shots
    assert abs(observed_prob - expected_prob) < tolerance, f"State {state}: probability {observed_prob:.3f} deviates too much from expected {expected_prob:.3f}"
```
